name: Tight
description: A compact line-height of 1.1–1.2 that pulls lines of text close together. Works well for headings and display type where tight optical spacing is desirable, but causes readability issues in running text.

name: Snug
description: Tailwind CSS's 'snug' value at 1.375. Slightly looser than tight, providing a comfortable feel for brief UI copy and short text blocks without wasting vertical space.

name: Normal
avoid_for: single-line headings where vertical rhythm is tightly controlled
description: Tailwind CSS's 'normal' value and the default browser line-height recommendation. At 1.5, it matches WCAG 1.4.8 body text requirements and provides comfortable reading for most content types.

name: Relaxed
description: Tailwind CSS's 'relaxed' value at 1.625. Provides extra breathing room between lines for comfortable extended reading. A good choice when readability and reader comfort are the primary goals.

name: Loose
avoid_for: any context where vertical space is limited
description: Tailwind CSS's 'loose' value at 2.0 (double-spaced). Maximum spacing between lines creates a very airy, open feel. Best reserved for poetry, code, or situations where maximum readability trumps space efficiency.

name: WCAG Minimum
description: The minimum line-height required by WCAG Success Criterion 1.4.8 (Visual Presentation) for body text. Ensures adequate spacing for users with cognitive or visual disabilities reading blocks of text.
